nodes.title:
General Community Property Principles

Marital economic community begins upon marriage and ends upon divorce, death, or
nodes.raw_text:
General Community Property Principles
•
Marital economic community begins upon marriage and ends upon divorce, death, or
permanent physical separation
•
CP: Acquired during marriage
•
SP:
•
Earned before marriage, during separation, or following divorce
•
Inheritances, gifts, devises, bequests acquired during marriage
•
QCP: Acquired by spouse/couple while domiciled in non-CP state that would have been CP if
acquired while domiciled in CA
nodes.normalized_text:
General Community Property Principles
•
Marital economic community begins upon marriage and ends upon divorce, death, or
permanent physical separation
•
community property: Acquired during marriage
•
separate property:
•
Earned before marriage, during separation, or following divorce
•
Inheritances, gifts, devises, bequests acquired during marriage
•
quasi-community property: Acquired by spouse/couple while domiciled in non-community property state that would have been community property if
acquired while domiciled in CA

rule_candidates.raw_rule_text:
General Community Property Principles
•
Marital economic community begins upon marriage and ends upon divorce, death, or
permanent physical separation
•
CP: Acquired during marriage
•
SP:
•
Earned before marriage, during separation, or following divorce
•
Inheritances, gifts, devises, bequests acquired during marriage
•
QCP: Acquired by spouse/couple while domiciled in non-CP state that would have been CP if
acquired while domiciled in CA
rule_candidates.normalized_rule_text:
General Community Property Principles
•
Marital economic community begins upon marriage and ends upon divorce, death, or
permanent physical separation
•
community property: Acquired during marriage
•
separate property:
•
Earned before marriage, during separation, or following divorce
•
Inheritances, gifts, devises, bequests acquired during marriage
•
quasi-community property: Acquired by spouse/couple while domiciled in non-community property state that would have been community property if
acquired while domiciled in CA

rule_candidates.node_title: Consent required for unilateral gift or sale of CP for less than FMV
rule_candidates.raw_rule_text:
Consent required for unilateral gift or sale of CP for less than FMV
•
If no consent, non-consenting spouse can ratify gift or revoke and sue to recover

•

rule_candidates.node_title: Sale or Lease of Community Real Property
rule_candidates.raw_rule_text:
Sale or Lease of Community Real Property
•
Sale or lease of CP real property for 1+ year requires written agreement signed by
both spouses

•

rule_candidates.node_title: SP = SP business’s value at time of marriage + (SP business’s value at time
rule_candidates.raw_rule_text:
SP = SP business’s value at time of marriage + (SP business’s value at time
of marriage × rate of return × years married)
rule_candidates.normalized_rule_text:
separate property = separate property business’s value at time of marriage + (separate property business’s value at time
of marriage × rate of return × years married)

rule_candidates.node_title: CP = the CP business’s value at time of separation + (value of CP at time
rule_candidates.raw_rule_text:
CP = the CP business’s value at time of separation + (value of CP at time
of separation x rate of return x years separated)
rule_candidates.normalized_rule_text:
community property = the community property business’s value at time of separation + (value of community property at time
of separation x rate of return x years separated)

rule_candidates.raw_rule_text:
CP contribution to SP
•
Community reimbursed or appreciation in property value, whichever is
greater
rule_candidates.normalized_rule_text:
community property contribution to separate property
•
Community reimbursed or appreciation in property value, whichever is
greater

rule_candidates.raw_rule_text:
Community entitled to reimbursement if
(1) CP or QCP funds used to pay for education
(2) Education substantially enhances spouse’s earning capacity, and
(3) No written agreement to the contrary
rule_candidates.normalized_rule_text:
Community entitled to reimbursement if
(1) community property or quasi-community property funds used to pay for education
(2) Education substantially enhances spouse’s earning capacity, and
(3) No written agreement to the contrary

rule_candidates.node_title: Cause of Action Against Third Party
rule_candidates.raw_rule_text:
Cause of Action Against Third Party
•
COA arose during marriage: Recovery is CP
•
Upon divorce: Recovery awarded to injured spouses unless (i) commingled funds or (ii) share
awarded to non-injured spouse in equity
•
COA arose before or after marriage: Recovery is SP
rule_candidates.normalized_rule_text:
Cause of Action Against Third Party
•
cause of action arose during marriage: Recovery is community property
•
Upon divorce: Recovery awarded to injured spouses unless (i) commingled funds or (ii) share
awarded to non-injured spouse in equity
•
cause of action arose before or after marriage: Recovery is separate property

rule_candidates.node_title: CP = (years between date hired and date separated) y [(years between date
rule_candidates.raw_rule_text:
CP = (years between date hired and date separated) y [(years between date
hired and date options become exercisable) x amount of shares]
rule_candidates.normalized_rule_text:
community property = (years between date hired and date separated) y [(years between date
hired and date options become exercisable) x amount of shares]

rule_candidates.node_title: CP = (years between date options were granted and date of separation) y
rule_candidates.raw_rule_text:
CP = (years between date options were granted and date of separation) y
[(years between date options were granted and date options become
exercisable) x amount of shares]
rule_candidates.normalized_rule_text:
community property = (years between date options were granted and date of separation) y
[(years between date options were granted and date options become
exercisable) x amount of shares]

rule_candidates.raw_rule_text:
Goodwill
•
If accumulated during marriage, goodwill is CP
•
For value, look to FMV or business’s excess earnings as result of goodwill

Goodwill
•
If accumulated during marriage, goodwill is community property
•
For value, look to fair market value or business’s excess earnings as result of goodwill

(2) Person had knowledge of activity, and
(3) Intent to advance activity

May search arrestee’s person and wingspan
Scope of Search – Homes
•
May search arrestee’s wingspan and conduct protective sweep where
accomplice may be hiding
Scope of Search – Automobiles
•
May search car only if arrestee is (i) unsecured and within reach of passenger
compartment or (ii) reasonable belief evidence contained therein

Revocable trust becomes irrevocable when grantor dies or loses capacity
TYPES OF TRUSTS

(3) At least 1 ascertainable beneficiary
(4) Valid trust purpose, and
(5) Trust res
